methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Prusa Research, a bootstrapped frontier-tech company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Josef Prusa, presents a valuation challenge due to limited comparable data at its specific stage and scale. The sector encompasses one public peer (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private competitors, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer universe does not support conventional comparable-company analysis.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable to this valuation. Of eleven same-sector peers identified in Robotics & Manufacturing, none satisfy the screening criteria for reliable triangulation: zero peers matched at the same stage, zero at an adjacent stage, and zero within a comparable valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, revenue scale, and capital structures across the broader robotics landscape.

Without sufficient same-stage or same-tier peer data, traditional valuation approaches cannot be meaningfully calibrated. Any credible range would require alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis grounded in Prusa's operating metrics, strategic buyer precedent transactions in manufacturing robotics, or revenue multiple inference from adjacent segments—none of which are present in the available data. The key uncertainty is the absence of comparable transactions or reliable public market anchors at Prusa's operational and financial scale.
